data.title: Buy Rating Underpinned by Tightening European Steel Supply and Underappreciated Earnings Upside
data.description:
Analyst Ioannis Masvoulas of Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on ArcelorMittal, with a price target of €46.20.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ioannis Masvoulas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to tightening steel supply and supportive pricing dynamics in Europe. The reported extended shutdown of the Avilés Blast Furnace B temporarily removes around 1% of regional capacity, but ArcelorMittal can largely compensate lost volumes through other plants, limiting operational disruption at the group level.

At the same time, this outage reinforces the trend toward a structurally undersupplied European steel market under the existing safeguard regime, which helps sustain higher marginal costs and firmer EU HRC price levels. On Masvoulas’s analysis, current spot pricing implies an annualised EBITDA notably above market consensus for 2026, suggesting that earnings expectations remain too conservative and that the stock offers attractive upside potential from its current valuation.

In another report released on February 13, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a €66.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Rebounding Water Heater Shipments Reinforce Bullish Outlook on A. O. Smith
data.description:
Stifel Nicolaus analyst Nathan Jones maintained a Buy rating on A. O. Smith Corporation on February 13 and set a price target of $85.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nathan Jones has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to strengthening end-market indicators for A. O. Smith. December AHRI data show a sharp rebound in U.S. residential water heater shipments, with both gas and electric units experiencing strong month-over-month and year-over-year gains, which supports a positive volume backdrop for the company’s North American business.

He also notes that total residential and commercial water heater shipments rose significantly on both a unit and an inflation-adjusted revenue basis, reinforcing the view that underlying demand is improving rather than just pricing. Given the historical statistical relationship between AHRI shipment-derived revenue trends and A. O. Smith’s organic growth in North America, these data points underpin confidence in the firm’s revenue outlook and support maintaining a constructive stance on the stock.

Jones covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Parker Hannifin, Lincoln Electric Holdings, and ITT. According to TipRanks, Jones has an average return of 24.2% and a 76.75% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on January 30, TipRanks – xAI also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $83.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
